name: El Mirador de la Cornisa, Refugi la Ferrería del Verdat – Parc de la Serralado Litoral – Circular Route
name_original: El Mirador de la Cornisa, Refugi la Ferrería del Verdat – Parc de la Serralado Litoral – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Premià De Dalt are situated in a small valley within the Serralada Litoral Natural Park, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region features extensive forests of pines and oaks, providing shaded trails and varied terrain. Joggers can experience routes that wind through hills, streams, and torrents, often with panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the surrounding mountains. Best jogging routes around Premià De Dalt The most popular jogging route is El Mirador de la Cornisa, Refugi la Ferrería del Verdat – Parc de la Serralado Litoral – Circular Route, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 47 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through the natural park with notable viewpoints. Another top favourite among local runners is Hípica Sant Mateu – Menhir Pedra del Diable loop from Premià de Dalt, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.7 km) path. This challenging loop explores the natural surroundings of Hípica Sant Mateu and passes by the ancient Menhir Pedra del Diable. Local runners also love the Hípica Sant Mateu – Font de Sant Mateu loop from Premià de Mar, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through the natural area around Hípica Sant Mateu and Font de Sant Mateu, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Jogging around Premià De Dalt is defined by the Serralada Litoral Natural Park, extensive pine and oak forests, and elevated trails offering sea views. The network of routes provides options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult, longer trails. The routes in Premià De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 6000 runners have used komoot to explore Premià De Dalt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Premià De Dalt?
A: Premià De Dalt offers a selection of around 15 dedicated running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, with options ranging from moderate to more challenging paths through the natural landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Premià De Dalt?
A: The jogging trails in Premià De Dalt feature a varied terrain, from flatter sections closer to the sea to more mountainous areas with hills, streams, and torrents. You'll primarily run through extensive forests of pines and oaks within the Serralada Litoral Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running routes suitable for beginners in Premià De Dalt?
A: Yes, there are several moderate running routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963585946/el-mirador-de-la-cornisa-refugi-la-ferreria-del-verdat-parc-de-la-serralado-litoral-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Mirador de la Cornisa, Refugi la Ferrería del Verdat – Parc de la Serralado Litoral – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile (4.9 km) trail that offers a pleasant run through the natural park with notable viewpoints.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Premià De Dalt?
A: The running routes in Premià De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, the panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and mountains, and the well-maintained trails through pine and oak forests.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced joggers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10485255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hípica Sant Mateu – Menhir Pedra del Diable loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.8-mile (20.7 km) path that explores the natural surroundings and passes by the ancient Menhir Pedra del Diable.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints of the sea or mountains?
A: Many trails in Premià De Dalt offer spectacular panoramic views. The region's location within the Serralada Litoral Natural Park means you'll often encounter elevated sections providing vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, the village, and the surrounding mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963585946/el-mirador-de-la-cornisa-refugi-la-ferreria-del-verdat-parc-de-la-serralado-litoral-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Mirador de la Cornisa, Refugi la Ferrería del Verdat – Parc de la Serralado Litoral – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its notable viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Premià De Dalt?
A: Yes, many of the running trails in Premià De Dalt are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31607335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hípica Sant Mateu – Font de Sant Mateu loop from Premià de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular 7.4-mile (11.9 km) circular trail.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while running in the area?
A: While running, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32190288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cadira del Bisbe loop from Premià de Mar&lt;/a&gt; passes near the Iberian Village of Cadira del Bisbe, an archaeological site. You might also spot various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1126577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1982187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Premià De Dalt suitable for families?
A: The varied terrain means some routes are more challenging, but there are moderate options that families with older children or those used to outdoor activities might enjoy. Always check the route's difficulty and distance to ensure it matches your family's fitness level. The natural park setting provides a great environment for outdoor family time.

Q: Is there public transport access to the jogging routes in Premià De Dalt?
A: Premià De Dalt is well-connected, and its proximity to larger cities like Barcelona suggests good public transport options. While specific route access points vary, you can generally reach the town center via public transport and then access trails within the Serralada Litoral Natural Park.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Hípica Sant Mateu?
A: Yes, Hípica Sant Mateu is a notable point of interest for runners. Both the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10485255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hípica Sant Mateu – Menhir Pedra del Diable loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31607335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hípica Sant Mateu – Font de Sant Mateu loop from Premià de Mar&lt;/a&gt; explore the natural surroundings of this area.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Premià De Dalt?
A: Thanks to its Mediterranean climate, Premià De Dalt offers pleasant conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in summer, the extensive pine and oak forests offer shaded trails, making it possible to run comfortably, especially during cooler morning or late afternoon hours.
tips:
This space for rest and privileged viewpoint at the foot of Sant Mateu, is enabled with benches and parking support for bikes, and car parking. There is a panoramic sign that locates, with the corresponding place names, the hills and peaks that surround this point of view, both in the Maresme and Barcelonès, and in the Vallès Oriental and Occidental. From this point you can see the mountains of Montjuïc and Tibidabo, La Conreria, the Teià valley and the hills on the Vallesana slope, as well as being able to contemplate Montserrat, La Mola and Sant Llorenç del Munt.
   The viewpoint was inaugurated in 2004.

It is a rectangular building that was used as a workshop where the tools used by the stonemasons were repaired and sharpened (basically, remaking the punches and scarps that the hard stone had worn away). In this area there was a great activity of stone extraction. Now the building is used as a shelter and is always open. It is necessary to request permission from the ADF and the local police to use the fireplace and to be able to eat or sleep when going with a gang.
The Font del Pericó d'en Canal is located in the Parque de la Cordillera Litoral. It is a modern font without any special charm, but very well done.
